title: Use readiness, liveness, and startup probes for distinct jobs
description: Readiness gates traffic, liveness restarts a hung container, startup protects slow boots; never reuse one endpoint for all three blindly.
body: Use when a pod serves traffic or has a non-trivial boot. Do not point liveness at a deep dependency check (a slow database makes liveness kill healthy pods in a restart storm), and do not omit readiness on a service that takes time to warm. Actions: set readiness to a lightweight self-check that flips false when the pod cannot serve; set liveness to detect only a wedged process; add startupProbe with a generous failureThreshold for slow boots. Validation signals include new pods receiving traffic only after readiness passes and no restart loops from liveness during dependency blips. Risk boundary: removing readiness from a live service is a traffic-correctness change needing review.
